---
title: "Foundation"
---
::: {.callout-tip collapse=true}
# Learning Goals: Foundation
**Course**

1. Know who your professor is  
2. Know how I will assess your mastery of course content

**Scientific Foundations**

1. Recognize that there are multiple scientifically valid ways to study human language; identify scientific evidence relevant to understanding (i) how languages are organized structurally, (ii) how humans perceive and process language in real time, and (iii) the brain basis of language
2. Define the following terms: *localization*, *lateralization*, *aphasia*
3. Explain the difference between *functional specialization* and *functional specificity*
4. Summarize the approach of 19th-century phrenology, identify unscientific aspects of this approach, and give an example of how this approach was used to cause societal harm 
5. Summarize the classical model of language in the brain and describe the original evidence for that model

**The toolbox**

1. Explain the logic of the lesion/deficit mapping method
2. Describe two methods that can be used to assess lateralization

**Claim-evidence mapping**

Describe scientific evidence for the following claim:  

1. Language processing in humans is lateralized to the left hemisphere.

**Brain Geography**

1. Be able to roughly locate Broca's area and Wernicke's area on a side view of the brain
2. Identify and distinguish *coronal*, *axial*, and *sagittal* views of the human brain; for each view, be able to tell which way the brain is "facing"
3. "Navigate" to a region of the brain using the following neuroanatomical and directional terms: *frontal*, *temporal*, *parietal*, *occipital*, *anterior*, *posterior*, *inferior*, *superior*, *lateral*, *medial*, *sulcus*, *gyrus*, *left*, *right*
:::




| Date | Class Topic | Due |
| -- | -- | -- | -- |
| Thurs 8/27 | [Syllabus](index.qmd), [assignments](requirements.qmd), [schedule](schedule.md) <br> Studying language; types of evidence [][1]{.slides} |  [SGN p. 1-9]{.reading} | 
| Tues 9/1 | Functional localization and phrenology [][2]{.slides} | [SGN p. 10-17]{.reading}  |
| Thurs 9/3 | Brain geography [][3]{.slides} |  [SGN p. 18-25]{.reading}
| Fri 9/4 || [](controversy.qmd#project-timeline){.controversy}
| Tues 9/8 | *No class (Monday schedule)* | | 
| Thurs 9/10 | Lesion/deficit mapping; classical model [][4]{.slides} | [SGN p. 27-31]{.reading} |
| Fri 9/11 || [](controversy.qmd#project-timeline){.controversy}
| Tues 9/15 | Sign language aphasia [][5]{.slides} | [Hickok et al. 1998]{.reading}
| Thurs 9/17 |  Lateralization [][6]{.slides} | [TBD]{.reading} |
| Tues 9/22 | [Exam #1]{.exam} |

{{< include _includes/legend.qmd >}}

[1]:https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1S-twq_R9KZWHKwUxCgpskvh7g66OaMdo1Z-OYOKt820/edit
[2]:https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1-DlaIOIvnQBYJNo8rJH51RvTGDmv6JFJAnk2MSbpzUQ/edit
[3]:https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1jyQqxVLcxgUTMdSuKAvVXysoGkv5phrmmoFLspAKKng/edit
[4]:https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1-ezBcrR7-fW8k9VVZgU28ZpjAFoxlYTB2a67PofzSqo/edit?slide=id.p#slide=id.p
[5]:https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1b5J07ecFhEIT58eD0hmq3WWEVxPnfBgvdifn58McDXs/edit?slide=id.p#slide=id.p
[6]: https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1AjYvk7ckzUOj0pFkItxFlR4eAeioK25fLUiPUwgR_9k/edit?slide=id.p#slide=id.p




**Resources**

* [Allen Brain Reference Atlases](https://atlas.brain-map.org/)
* [Neurotorium Brain Atlas](https://neurotorium.org/tool/brain-atlas/)
* [UBC: The Cortex](https://www.neuroanatomy.ca/modules/Cortex/story.html)
* [Dichotic listening task](https://learninglink.oup.com/access/content/sedivy-2e-student-resources/sedivy2e-chapter-3-web-activity-1?previousFilter=tag_chapter-03)
* [UBC Aphasia Videos](https://neuroanatomy.ca/modules/Language/story.html)
